exemplar.title: To what extent did Old Spiceâs 2010 advertisement, âThe Man Your Man Could Smell Like, â influence the companyâs sales performance and enhance customer engagement with the brand?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent focus on the key concept of change throughout methodology, tools and conclusion
Clear, organization-specific examples linking Old Spiceâs history, ownership and metrics to the concept
weaknesses: Several SWOT sections describe factors without explicit reference back to change
Early background narrative does not integrate the concept fully into the introduction
Concept wording appears in headings more than in analytical paragraphs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand feedback shows strong understanding of the concept (band 4) and an excellent organizational connection (band 5), but integration is not fully sustained in every analytical paragraph. A midâlevel mark of 3 reflects good conceptual framing with room to weave 'change' more deeply into each section.

strengths: Provides exactly three clearly labeled supporting documents fitting the 3â5 requirement
Documents are directly relevant to Old Spiceâs campaign and include key statistics (e.g. sales rise, social-media growth)
weaknesses: Sources are mainly blogs and LinkedIn posts with limited depth and academic rigor
Insights from documents are not consistently integrated into the main analysis
Lacks benchmarking or triangulation to deepen evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although quantity meets band 3, the sources lack depth and breadth. Assigning 2 reflects relevance but acknowledges that documents are not sufficiently in-depth or varied to support higher marks.

strengths: Use of two classic strategic tools (Ansoff Matrix and SWOT) clearly outlined in the methodology
Both tools are applied to the research question with direct links to Old Spiceâs market activity
weaknesses: Limited selectionâno additional financial or quantitative models beyond the two tools
Ansoff Diversification quadrant left blank, missing analysis
Application sometimes remains descriptive rather than deeply analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and application meet an adequate standard (band 3) but lack justification for tool choice and complete application. A score of 2 captures effective use with notable gaps.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Incorporates quantitative data points (e.g. 125% sales increase, 2700% Twitter growth) linked to analysis
Draws some connections between the advert and marketâpenetration/product-development strategies
weaknesses: Reliance on secondary data without assessing reliability or validity
SWOT and tool outputs often remain descriptive; limited synthesis across tools
Evaluation sections miss long-term implications and critical depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand comments indicate some analysis of the research question (band 3) but largely descriptive evaluation. A mark of 2 reflects use of data and initial analysis while acknowledging limited critical synthesis.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question about sales and engagement impact
Summarizes key evidence (sales growth, social-media metrics) clearly
weaknesses: Introduces new claims on sustainability without support from prior analysis
Lacks deeper strategic recommendations or linkage back to conceptual framework
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence alignment is partial (band 2) and the research question is answered explicitly (band 3). Balancing these, a score of 2 reflects a coherent conclusion but flags unsupported new claims.

strengths: Clear signposting of sections (introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion)
Logical overall flow with a table of contents included
weaknesses: Opportunities section ends mid-sentence, indicating missing content
Research question formatting split disrupts clarity
Table of contents omits page numbers, affecting navigability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the structure is generally appropriate (band 2), missing content and formatting issues compromise coherence. A mark of 1 reflects the need for a more polished, complete layout.

strengths: Includes all required elements: title page, word count, references, supporting documents
Adheres to the word limit of 1800 words
weaknesses: Figure captions are generic and not descriptive
SWOT table misalignments hurt readability
Bibliography entries inconsistent in style and missing dates
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met (band 2), but formatting inconsistencies and alignment problems justify reducing to a score of 1, reflecting a need for improved visual clarity and citation consistency.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structure (introduction, methodology, analysis, tools, conclusion) and includes a table of contents. Headings are clear and material is sequenced coherently, so an âappropriate structure throughoutâ is demonstrated.

The documents are directly about Old Spiceâs campaign and contain relevant statistics (e.g. 2700 % Twitter growth, 125 % sales rise). However, the sources are mainly blogs and LinkedIn articles with limited depth or triangulation, so they cannot be considered âin-depth and wide-rangingâ. This fits ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the highest quality.

All required presentation elementsâtitle page with word count, table of contents, references, supporting documentsâare present, and the work stays within the 1800-word limit. Hence the strand meets the full requirement.
Exactly three supporting documents are provided and clearly labelled. This satisfies the requirement of providing 3-5 documents and fits the 3-mark band for quantity.

Both tools are applied to the research question: the Ansoff discussion links product launches and market penetration to sales, and the SWOT is interpreted in the context of the 2010 advert. The relevance is clear, yet the application sometimes slips into description (e.g. listing social-media statistics without deeper interpretation), so the work is âadequately appliedâ rather than âclear and effectiveâ.

The student quotes several quantitative indicatorsâsales up 125 %, market-share doubling, social-media metricsâand inserts a comparative table. The data are drawn from secondary sources and not always critically assessed, yet they are adequate and clearly linked to points in the analysis, matching the 3-mark descriptor for selection and use.

The narrative goes beyond mere description by relating the advert to market penetration, product development and long-term brand equity; however, large parts of the SWOT remain descriptive and there is limited synthesis between tools. This equates to âsome analysis of the research questionâ rather than mostly effective integration.

The research question is directly answeredâOld Spiceâs 2010 advert significantly boosted sales and customer engagementâwithin the dedicated conclusion section. The response is explicit, satisfying the top band for this strand.

Two classic strategic tools, Ansoff Matrix and SWOT, are selected. This is an adequate but not extensive toolkit; there is no financial ratio, Boston Matrix, or other quantitative model. The choice therefore meets âadequate selectionâ at the 3-mark level.

The conclusion restates key findings (sales growth, engagement metrics) and they are generally aligned with evidence presented, yet new claims such as future sustainability needs are introduced without supporting data. This partial alignment corresponds to âsome conclusions consistent with evidenceâ (2 marks).

Throughout the IA the student anchors each argument firmly to Old Spice: historical background, ownership by P&G, specific sales figures, social-media metrics and product launches are all related back to the firmâs strategic position. Every analytical tool (Ansoff, SWOT) is applied directly to the organisation, and the conclusion explicitly returns to what the findings mean for Old Spice. This represents a comprehensive, fully integrated connection to the organisation, meriting the top band.

The student consistently frames the whole investigation around the key concept of change, repeatedly relating Old Spiceâs repositioning and re-branding to the need for strategic change. Links to change are made in the Introduction, the methodology discussion, the Ansoff-matrix narrative and the conclusion. Nevertheless, references to âchangeâ are not woven into every analytical paragraph (e.g. large sections of the SWOT simply describe strengths and threats without explicit reference to change). This demonstrates a strong understanding with partial, but not full, integration, which aligns best with the 4-mark descriptor.

annotations.comment: Figure caption âStudent-made SWOT analysisâ is generic. Use a concise, descriptive caption that reflects key insights.
Weaknesses section notes reliance on viral success but omits recommendations for mitigation. Suggest strategic safeguards.
The Opportunities narrative ends mid-sentence (âOld Spiceâ), indicating missing content. Complete the analysis for coherence.
The conclusion introduces climate sustainability without prior analysis. Avoid new claims; base conclusions solely on presented evidence.
Introduction section is clearly signposted and logically placed, supporting coherent structure.
Word count of 1736 remains within the 1800-word limit. The student has adhered to presentation requirements.
Table of contents lists all sections but omits page numbers. Including page references would improve navigability.
The student correctly identifies âChangeâ as the key concept, but could strengthen integration by referencing it not only in headings but explicitly within the introductionâs narrative.
The research question is split into two text blocks, which disrupts clarity. Combine and format it as a single coherent sentence.
The 2700% Twitter interaction claim is impressive but lacks evaluation of data reliability. Critically assess the validity of such metrics.
Market Development discussion notes appeal to women but does not quantify its impact. Include data on femaleâled purchases or engagement.
The opening background describes Old Spice history but does not yet reference the key concept of change. Early integration would heighten conceptual focus.
Methodology describes chosen tools but lacks justification of why Ansoff and SWOT directly address the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Relying mainly on blogs and LinkedIn articles limits source depth. Incorporate industry reports or academic studies for stronger evidence.
The Ansoff matrix table leaves the âDiversificationâ cell blank, missing analysis of that quadrant. Populate and interpret all cells.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Market Penetration section describes tactics but does not reference the 125% sales increase. Tie analytical narrative to quantitative data explicitly.
Product development narrative lists new launches but does not tie them back to the 2010 campaignâs role in enabling these products.
The Diversification section states no immediate diversification but offers no analysis of long-term strategic implications. Expand evaluation.
SWOT strengths include âHigh Marketing Return on Investmentâ but lack ROI metrics. Provide numeric ROI figures to substantiate this point.
The SWOT tableâs column headings and cells are misaligned, affecting readability. Reformat for consistent alignment.
Opportunities section mentions future entry into skin care but misses supporting data. Reference specific evidence or market statistics.
Threats section is comprehensive but descriptive. Prioritize threats by potential impact, using data to evaluate their severity.
The conclusion explicitly answers the research question by summarizing the advertâs impact on sales and engagement.
Bibliography entries vary in style and omit dates in some cases. Apply a consistent citation format throughout.
Supporting Document #1 overview describes market competition but is not linked back to analytical sections. Integrate its insights into analysis.
Supporting Document #2 provides extensive statistics but lacks benchmarking against industry averages. Contextualize metrics for depth.
Supporting Document #3 traces brand history well but offers limited performance data. Select documents with quantitative sales or engagement metrics.
